title: Aluminum Price Falls to $3,530 Amid Supply Disruptions
summary: Aluminum futures in the UK fell toward $3,530 per tonne, extending their retreat from a four-year high despite renewed concerns over supply disruptions from the Persian Gulf. Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz again only hours after reopening it, blaming the US for its ongoing blockade of Iranian ports. The region accounts for roughly 9% of global output.

title: Global Aluminum Prices Consolidate at $3,555.13 Amid Historic Supply Shock
summary: Global aluminum prices are consolidating at $3,555.13 per ton, up marginally 0.08%, as the market digests a historic supply shock anchored by extreme geopolitical disruptions. Elevated levels are driven by the ongoing maritime blockade in the Strait of Hormuz and targeted strikes crippling Middle Eastern smelting capacity at major EGA and Alba facilities. China's prices remain firm at ¥24,233 per ton, solidifying expectations of a prolonged global structural deficit.

title: World's Top Aluminium Smelter Cuts Output Due to Shipping Chaos
summary: The world's largest single-site aluminium smelter in Bahrain, Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), cut its output by about 20% due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This shutdown affects three reduction lines, accounting for 19% of Alba's 1.62 million tons annual capacity, or roughly 2.2% of global aluminium production, to preserve raw material inventory. The cutback risks tightening global supply chains and pushing aluminium prices higher.

title: The Aluminum Crisis No One Sees Coming - (it changes everything)
summary: Global aluminum supply faces a crisis as China's production nears its 45 million tons annual capacity cap imposed in 2017, with no other regions filling the gap; Europe has reduced capacity by 40% since 2008, and LME stocks have dropped sharply to under 300,000 tons by late 2025. US LME warehouses hold zero tons, with only one restart announced: Century Aluminum's 50,000 tons at Mount Holly by June 2026. A potential 3-month disruption could remove 1-1.25 million tons of primary production.

title: Aluminium deficit will support prices in 2026
summary: China's aluminium output is approaching its 2017 self-imposed 45 million tonne capacity cap, reducing net exports by 700kt year-to-date and tightening markets outside China. In the US, Century Aluminium plans to restart 50 ktpa of idled capacity at Mount Holly smelter by Q2 2026.

title: Hammerer Aluminium Industries Acquires Stake in Aluminij Industries
summary: Hammerer Aluminium Industries (HAI) acquired a stake in Aluminij Industries, marking a strategic milestone in its growth and sustainability strategy. This move supports advancements in aluminum technology and sustainable practices within the industry.

title: apt Group Achieves Milestone in Aluminum Battery Housings with Friction Stir Welding
summary: The apt Group, a leading provider of aluminum solutions, partnered with RRS Schilling GmbH to introduce friction stir welding (FSW) for producing aluminum battery housings for agricultural machinery. This innovation enhances manufacturing efficiency and sustainability in transport and industrial sectors.

title: New Downstream Aluminum Fabrication Facility Planned in Inola, Oklahoma
summary: A new facility in Inola, Oklahoma, is planned to convert molten primary aluminum into value-added products. This development supports innovation in aluminum processing and potential sustainability improvements through advanced fabrication techniques.

title: Aluminium Scrap Market Set to Surge to US$ 172.2 Billion by 2033
summary: The aluminium scrap market is projected to reach US$ 172.2 billion by 2033, driven by the circular economy and production constraints. Asia Pacific leads with 39% share due to expansion in China and India, policy support, and rising recycling demand, promoting sustainability.

title: New Aluminum-Thermal System Unlocks Carbon-Free Industrial Heat and Hydrogen
summary: A new aluminum-thermal system leverages the metal's energy density to provide carbon-free industrial heat and hydrogen, replacing fossil fuels. This innovation advances sustainable aluminum technology in industrial applications.

title: Hydro's Roadmap to Net-Zero Emissions in Aluminium Production
summary: Hydro commits to net-zero emissions in aluminum production by 2050 using cutting-edge technology, renewable energy, and increased recycling of post-consumer scrap. This reduces the carbon footprint to one-fourth of the global average, emphasizing sustainability innovations.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3135 CNY/T on April 21, 2026, down 0.10% from the previous day, though still up 3.91% over the past year. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,100 per ton to a two-week high due to firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton for next month, its fifth consecutive increase.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel increased to 1075.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024, with a 4.99% gain over the past 4 weeks. It rose to 1,104.97 USD/T on April 20, up 0.27% from the previous day. This reflects strengthening market trends in hot-rolled coil steel.

title: AISI: Raw steel production strengthens further
summary: Domestic mill production reached a new multi-year high last week per AISI figures. Cleveland-Cliffs reported improving supply-demand fundamentals in Q1 2026 and expects stronger conditions ahead. Nucor raised its hot-rolled coil spot price to $1,055 per short ton, up $10 from last week.

title: U.S. Steel Price Outlook: HRC Climbs to $1,055, Production Hits Four-Year High, and Imports Down
summary: HRC steel prices climbed to $1,055, with U.S. production hitting a four-year high and imports declining. The video provides outlook on these strengthening market trends. Published within the last 24 hours.

title: US raw steel production increases by 0.3 percent - week 16, 2026
summary: US raw steel production saw a slight increase of 0.3 percent for week 16 of 2026. This update reflects recent domestic production trends amid ongoing industry monitoring. The report is part of SteelOrbis's latest steel news coverage.

title: India’s JSW Steel approves JV with POSCO to build 6 million mt steel mill in Odisha
summary: JSW Steel has approved a joint venture with POSCO to construct a 6 million metric ton steel mill in Odisha, India. This development aims to expand production capacity in the region. The announcement highlights significant investment in new steelmaking infrastructure.

title: Steel production in China fell by 4.6% y/y in Q1
summary: Steel production in China declined by 4.6% year-over-year during the first quarter. This drop indicates challenges in the world's largest steel producer amid market conditions. The news is part of GMK Center's daily steel market updates.

title: Lloyds Metals & Energy Turns to Metso for Filtration Technology
summary: Metso Corp. has been selected by Lloyds Metals & Energy Ltd. for filtration technology implementation. This represents a recent advancement in steel production technology aimed at improving operational efficiency.

title: The Impact of Geopolitical Conflict on Ferrous Scrap and Steel Markets
summary: Industry experts discuss how geopolitical tensions are impacting ferrous scrap availability, steel pricing, and logistics. The webinar highlights freight and logistics disruptions as major pressure points affecting the steel supply chain and procurement challenges.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its El Teniente smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development is relevant to copper market supply dynamics as El Teniente is a major copper production facility.

title: Copper price movement - April 21, 2026
summary: Copper fell to $6.01 USD/Lbs on April 21, 2026, down 0.39% from the previous day. Over the past month, copper prices have risen 10.53%, and are up 23.20% compared to the same period last year.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Reports 71,417 Tonnes of Copper in Anode Produced by Kamoa-Kakula in Q1 2026; Recovery Efforts Advancing
summary: Ivanhoe Mines announced production of 71,417 tonnes of copper in anode by the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Complex in Q1 2026. Africa's largest copper smelter has ramped up to 60% capacity, with improving sales of 99.7%-pure anodes and by-product high-strength sulphuric acid. The Phase 1, 2, and 3 concentrators are set to reach a combined steady-state rate of 17 million tonnes per annum, with revised guidance for over 500,000 tonnes of copper per annum from 2028.

title: Zambia's copper smelters plan extended shutdowns
summary: Copper smelters in Zambia, Africa's No.2 producer of the metal vital for clean energy, are planning extended shutdowns. This development could impact regional copper production capacity. The article is part of ongoing coverage on global mining news.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to 18,235 USD/T on April 20, 2026, up 1.39% from the previous day, reaching the highest since March 2026 amid cost pressures and supply constraints. Indonesia revised its nickel ore benchmark pricing formula, incorporating additional metals and raising the corrective factor, tightening market conditions. Prices have stabilized in the $17,000–$17,400 range due to elevated inventories and projected 2026 surplus, though policy support in Western Australia aids production.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Nickel prices advanced by 1.06% to $18,167.64/ton globally and ¥123,829/ton in China, driven by Indonesia's revised nickel ore benchmark pricing that raises production costs for smelters. Heightened Middle East geopolitical tensions threaten sulfur imports vital for Indonesia's HPAL sector, adding supply risks. A softer US dollar and stabilizing Chinese stainless steel demand support the firmer market outlook.

title: Indonesia nickel processing utilization to drop amid lower nickel ore
summary: Indonesia's nickel industry group FINI stated that 2026 nickel processing production capacity utilization could drop to as low as 70% due to a lower nickel ore production quota. Utilization is expected to fall to 70-75% from 90% last year, while nickel ore imports are anticipated to surge. FINI chairman Arif Perdana Kusumah shared this with Reuters.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine, PT Weda Bay Nickel in Indonesia, has been notified of a drastic cut in ore production quota to 12 million tons for this year, down from 42 million tons in 2025. This move aims to boost global prices of nickel, Indonesia's key export commodity. The company intends to apply for a revision.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia announced a 2026 nickel ore production target of 250-260 million tons, flat to down from 2025, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton amid surging EV demand. EV markets show 20% underlying growth, with analysts forecasting 6-7% annual nickel demand growth. Production discipline from Indonesia is key to price direction.
